metadata.title: PQCToday Compliance Assessment Plan

roles.title: Subject-Matter Expert Reviewer
roles.description: Domain expert who reviews and approves data-record changes per the trust-engine peer-review workflow (see explainability doc §6).

roles.title: Editorial Board
roles.description: Maintainers responsible for cross-domain consistency, vocabulary normalisation, and bypass-detection oversight.

roles.title: Automated Extraction (LLM)
roles.description: qwen3.6:27b runs locally via Ollama to extract enrichments from cached source documents. Outputs are cross-checked (N20/N21 validators) before SME review.

parties.name: PQC Today Platform Team
parties.remarks: Maintainers of the trust-engine pipeline; merge-gate authority for non-SME-domain changes.

parties.name: [reviewer ericamador]
parties.remarks: Subject-matter expert. Domains: all. Active since 2026-05-08.

metadata.remarks: Methodology document describing how the trust-engine pipeline produces the assessment-results emitted by scripts/generate-oscal.ts. Generated by scripts/generate-oscal-assessment-plan.ts (T14).

import-ssp.remarks: No system-security-plan is published — the assessment is over a public data corpus, not a controlled system. This stub satisfies the OSCAL schema requirement.

control-selections.remarks: All compliance records in compliance_*.csv are in scope. The governance subset (records with cswp39_tags or governance body type) is the headline focus; the full inventory is available via the -full export per FR-OSCAL-13.

assessment-subjects.description: Compliance frameworks tracked in src/data/compliance_*.csv — each row is one OSCAL inventory-item. The latest dated CSV is authoritative; older versions are archived in src/data/archive/ per CSVmaintenance.md.

IR 8477 concept-xwalk graph (concept_xwalks_*.csv) provides the typed semantic edges between compliance records, library standards, and timeline events. Exported as part of the trust-engine evidence chain, not as an OSCAL component per se.

assessment-platforms.title: PQC Today Hub
assessment-platforms.remarks: React SPA at https://pqctoday.com — the public surface. The trust-engine pipeline (qwen3.6:27b enrichment, cross-checks, peer-review CI workflows, ML-DSA-65 attestation of revisions.jsonl) lives in the pqctoday-hub repo.

tasks.title: qwen3.6:27b extraction + cross-check
tasks.description: Local Ollama extraction populates per-document enrichments. enrichment-crosscheck.ts (N20/N21) compares LLM output against TF-IDF source passages. Failed cross-checks block merge.

tasks.title: SME peer review (GitHub PR or offline attestation)
tasks.description: Each cross-checked change is reviewed by an SME from public/data/reviewers.json. GitHub-handle-bearing SMEs review on the PR; offline SMEs send attestations via the approvals/offline-{pr_number}-{reviewer_id}.json mechanism (see PRD FR-D-09).

tasks.title: Integrity validator suite (~120 checks)
tasks.description: scripts/validate-data-integrity.ts runs the full check suite (CM-W, CM-C, CM-T, CM-G, CM-TS, CM-AT, MR-1, QA-S, QA-CSWP, N1..N21, etc.). Any ERROR-severity finding blocks merge.

tasks.title: Append revisions.jsonl + sign with ML-DSA-65
tasks.description: On merge, scripts/ci/append-revision.ts appends a structured revision record. .github/workflows/sign-revisions.yml then signs revisions.jsonl with the maintainer ML-DSA-65 (FIPS 204) attestation key (T12). Detached signature lives next to the file.

resources.title: Trust Engine Explainability Document
resources.description: Narrative companion describing the eight scoring dimensions, IR 8477 / CSWP 39 binding, validator suite, hybrid LLM + cross-check, and UI surfaces.

resources.title: Trust Engine Implementation Plan
resources.description: ICE-ranked task list (T01–T23) with enrichment-dependency labels (INDEP / HELPS-ENRICH / POST-ENRICH).

resources.title: Maintainer Attestation Key Management Policy
resources.description: Operational policy for the ML-DSA-65 maintainer key (generation, rotation, compromise, verification).
